summary:
There are several gas stations arranged in a circle. The gas[i] array gives the fuel available at each station, and cost[i] gives the fuel needed to drive from station i to the next one. You drive clockwise with an empty tank and unlimited capacity.

Return the index of the station from which you can start and make a full loop back to it. If no such start exists, return -1. When a solution exists it is guaranteed to be unique.

examples.explanation: Starting at station 3 lets the tank stay non-negative through every leg of the circuit.
Total fuel is less than total cost, so the loop can never be completed from any station.

constraints: If an answer exists, it is unique
